mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly compressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above trend fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 60-day returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate supply-side contraction and weakening manufacturing activity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Two-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL acceleration fails to predict negative 20-day returns during tightening cycles, with positive returns exceeding 55 percent of cases.
